camera.notes: Orthographic package smoke camera over the cooked 2D scene.

inputMap.purpose: Move the player sprite through deterministic 2D gameplay smoke rows.
Exercise audio, contact, and animation feedback in the package proof.

coreLoop.objective: Move the cooked player sprite through the packaged scene while package-visible systems report deterministic gameplay counters.
coreLoop.feedback: Sprite animation, HUD submission, runtime UI workbench rows, audio cue, physics contacts, navigation, AI, and simulation counters are emitted by selected package recipes.
coreLoop.winState: Installed package recipes report clean 2D package, gameplay systems, runtime UI workbench, native sprite, and simulation evidence.
coreLoop.failState: Missing package files, invalid scene references, or unsupported broad claims fail schema/static/package validation.
coreLoop.restart: Rerun the package smoke from the manifest-derived runtime package files without mutating cooked payloads.

sceneList.purpose: Desktop runtime configuration shipped through runtimePackageFiles.
Package index used by installed validation and runtime scene loading.

assetRequests.purpose: Reviewed RGBA8 source frames feed first-party sprite atlas authoring before cooked runtime consumption.
First-party tilemap metadata drives deterministic visible tile counters.
Cooked UI atlas metadata proves image and glyph atlas palette handoff counters before renderer submission.
Cooked audio cue proves device-independent audio feedback.

qualityGates.evidence: Installed package validation loads manifest-derived config, package index, and cooked scene payloads.
Gameplay systems, native sprite, UI, audio, physics, navigation, and AI counters are selected package evidence.
RPG systems smoke reports deterministic stat, progression, skill, equipment, combat-loop intent, reward, save-validation, replay-hash, and zero side-effect counters.

Sandbox world smoke reports deterministic chunk, resident chunk, existing cell, placement/destruction intent, cost, mutation, persistence, repairable persistence, unsafe rejection, replay-hash, and zero world/persistence/package side-effect counters.

Simulation management smoke reports deterministic resource balance, job assignment, logistics transfer, economy summary, population need, schedule, save-review, dashboard, replay-hash, and zero side-effect counters.

Network replication smoke reports network_replication_status=host_evidence_required, network_replication_reviewed=1, network_replication_ready=0, network_replication_object_rows=2, network_replication_input_rows=2, network_replication_snapshot_rows=2, network_replication_rollback_rows=1, network_replication_transport_host_evidence=0, network_replication_diagnostics=0, positive replay-hash, and zero network/world/rollback side-effect counters.

Network production security smoke reports deterministic network_production_security_* fields for a reviewed threat model, session lifecycle, connection state, channel policy, sequence/replay, input, snapshot, and rollback-window evidence rows, host-gated loopback/replication readiness, zero external network/thread/save/world side effects, clean unsupported-online-claim rows, and a positive replay hash without claiming matchmaking, NAT traversal, encryption/authentication, cloud backend, or broad online readiness.
Runtime menu HUD smoke reports deterministic display, command, dialogue, and input-binding prompt counters.

Runtime UI workbench smoke reports deterministic menu, inventory, equipment, shop, simulation dashboard table and graph, focus navigation, text input, localization identity, and accessibility identity counters.

Runtime UI production stack smoke reports side-effect-free shaping direction/script/language rows, glyph ids/clusters/advances/offsets/fallback/bidi/line-break evidence, glyph bitmap/metrics/pixel-format rows, selected package counters, host gates, zero dependency-gated/skipped/adapter-invoked/unsupported rows, and a positive replay hash without claiming broad production UI readiness.

Runtime UI renderer atlas handoff smoke loads cooked GameEngine.UiAtlas.v1 metadata from runtimePackageFiles, builds image and glyph palettes, submits one glyph and one image through MK_ui_renderer, then reports selected package counters without source image decoding, live glyph atlas generation, renderer upload APIs, native handles, or broad UI rendering claims.
Audio gameplay mixer smoke reads the cooked package audio payload, then reports deterministic bus, cue, trigger, command, pause, fade, loop, spatial, render, and sample diagnostic counters.

Audio production smoke reports audio_production_* fields for reviewed decoded source, streaming chunk, conversion, DSP, spatial/listener, HRTF host-gate, WASAPI device lifecycle, unsupported-claim, side-effect, diagnostic, and replay-hash evidence. It remains host-gated for device and HRTF proof and does not claim broad codec support, middleware parity, HRTF execution parity, native handles, background streaming execution, or general production audio quality.

Source image/audio codec review smoke reports source_image_audio_codec_review_status=ready, six reviewed rows, exact vcpkg.libspng and vcpkg.miniaudio dependency/legal records, PNG RGBA8 pixel format evidence, audio sample-format evidence, selected package evidence, zero unsupported claims, zero SVG/background/HRTF/runtime-source/native/package side effects, and a positive replay hash without broad image or audio codec readiness.
Simulation orchestration smoke reports fixed-step command playback and clean diagnostics without running broad game rules.

aiOwnedSourceRoots.evidence: Generated game-owned files are confined to this root; shared repository surfaces require a separate developer-owned task.
Runtime package payload changes must stay manifest-listed and validation-recipe-backed.
Generated source authoring fixtures remain game-local before reviewed cook/package handoff.
Generated shader source fixtures remain game-local and require shader-toolchain validation before package claims.

forbiddenSharedPaths.reason: Engine internals require a separate developer-owned capability task.
Editor internals are outside generated-game mutation scope.
Shared automation changes require a repository workflow task.
Shared schema changes require explicit agent-surface synchronization.
Machine-readable engine contracts are composed from reviewed fragments only.
CI workflow changes are outside generated-game mutation scope.
Plan and production-ledger changes are developer-owned repository work.
Shared game registration is changed only by reviewed scaffolding tools.

remediationActions.trigger: A manifest-declared runtime package payload is missing or not registered.
remediationActions.expectedResult: Repair only game-local runtime payload registration before rerunning package validation.

remediationActions.trigger: A requested change targets a forbidden shared repository path.
remediationActions.expectedResult: Stop and record a developer-owned handoff instead of editing shared surfaces.

remediationActions.trigger: A requested game behavior needs an unsupported reusable engine capability.
remediationActions.expectedResult: Persist a reviewed aiWorkflow.engineCapabilityHandoffs row with first-party public-contract evidence.

remediationActions.trigger: A selected validation recipe fails after generated game-local mutation.
remediationActions.expectedResult: Repair game-owned rows without weakening validation, deleting evidence, or changing shared tooling.

reviewedToolSurfaces.evidence: Plans deterministic first-party placeholder source documents and provenance rows without external asset downloads.
Routes placeholder source documents through registered source cook/package planning before runtime package consumption.

packageHandoff.evidence: Package handoff stays manifest-listed, first-party, and validation-recipe-backed before runtime use.

replacementWorkflow.evidence: Placeholder replacement must keep the stable asset key, regenerate or review a game-owned source document, preserve provenance/license rows, and reroute through reviewed cook/package handoff before runtime use.

reviewedRecipeSurfaces.evidence: Plans allowlisted validation recipe argv and host gates without evaluating raw manifest command strings.
Executes only reviewed validation recipes while preserving host-gate decisions and recipe output summaries.
Reviews package smoke stdout, gameplay counters, and diagnostics before choosing mutation-ledger remediation.

failureClassifications.trigger: A selected recipe reports a missing manifest-listed runtimePackageFiles payload or package artifact.
failureClassifications.evidence: Repair the game-owned package file or manifest registration through reviewed package surfaces.

failureClassifications.trigger: A manifest, package index, scene, asset, or recipe reference points at an undeclared or invalid row.
failureClassifications.evidence: Repair game-owned descriptor rows without deleting validation evidence.

failureClassifications.trigger: A recipe requires host gates that are unavailable or unacknowledged on the current machine.
failureClassifications.evidence: Record the host gate and do not bypass it or substitute an unrelated ready claim.

failureClassifications.trigger: A selected shader or package recipe reports missing D3D12, Vulkan, Metal, or shader toolchain evidence.
failureClassifications.evidence: Stop at a developer-owned toolchain or engine capability handoff when the game cannot repair the gap.

failureClassifications.trigger: Package stdout lacks expected gameplay, renderer, UI, audio, physics, navigation, or AI counters.
failureClassifications.evidence: Repair game-owned rows or code through reviewed mutation surfaces without weakening expected counters.

failureClassifications.trigger: Runtime package load, index, asset payload, or runtime scene validation fails.
failureClassifications.evidence: Repair reviewed runtime package files and package index rows before rerunning smoke recipes.

failureClassifications.trigger: A remediation would touch shared repository paths, engine internals, raw shell, or unreviewed cooked packages.
failureClassifications.evidence: Stop at mutation-ledger remediation or developer-owned handoff instead of broadening game write scope.

remediationPolicy.evidence: All repair actions route through aiWorkflow.contentMutationLedger remediation rows and reviewed game-local command surfaces.

requiredEvidence: missing runtimePackageFiles row or package artifact path
stopConditions: target path leaves the game root

requiredEvidence: invalid manifest, package, scene, asset, or recipe reference
host gate id and unavailable host tool or device
stopConditions: an unrelated ready claim would replace host evidence

requiredEvidence: missing shader toolchain or backend evidence
stopConditions: engine internals or toolchain policy must change

requiredEvidence: expected gameplay, renderer, UI, audio, physics, navigation, or AI counter
package load, index, asset payload, or runtime scene validation diagnostic
stopConditions: package repair needs unreviewed cooked artifact mutation
runtime source parsing would be introduced

requiredEvidence: forbidden shared path or unsafe mutation request
stopConditions: shared repository files would be edited from the game lane
raw shell or native handles would be exposed

evidence: aiWorkflow.gameDesignSpec.inputMap declares player actions and default bindings
selected package smokes report gameplay, renderer, UI, audio, physics, navigation, or AI feedback counters
validation remediation rows keep rerun-selected-validation-recipe policy
desktop runtime release target and selected installed package smoke are manifest-declared deterministic recipes
selected budget smoke reports package-visible entity scale, culling, draw, update, or frame budget evidence

unsupportedRows.reason: Rubric rows prove declared objective and deterministic evidence only.
No commercial-quality, balance, or content-market fitness claim is made.
Host-gated backend and platform evidence stays per recipe.
Budget rows are selected package evidence, not unbounded performance proof.
Only reviewed first-party/generated package content can satisfy rubric evidence.
The rubric does not tune or rebalance gameplay autonomously.
Rubric evidence uses manifest rows and first-party counters only.
Failing rubric rows route to remediation without weakening selected validation recipes.
This is a generated-game foundation rubric, not a broad production readiness claim.

gameplayContract.physics: mirakana::PhysicsWorld2D deterministic contact and trigger counters
gameplayContract.renderer: mirakana::IRenderer from the desktop host with deterministic NullRenderer fallback or host-owned RHI-backed presentation when packaged shader artifacts are present
gameplayContract.spriteAnimationFlipbook: advance_runtime_sprite_flipbook_playback selects RuntimeSpriteFlipbookDirectionSetRow rows by gameplay state and direction, applies RuntimeSpriteFlipbookPlaybackMode loop/clamp policy, reports RuntimeSpriteFlipbookEventDesc rows, and emits sprite_flipbook_direction_sets, sprite_flipbook_event_rows, sprite_flipbook_playback_modes, sprite_flipbook_gameplay_state_rows, sprite_flipbook_events_sampled, and sprite_flipbook_playback_diagnostics through --require-sprite-animation
gameplayContract.currentRuntime: host-gated Win32 desktop runtime package proof for 2D gameplay. D3D12 package window smoke uses packaged DXIL shader bytecode and --require-d3d12-shaders --require-d3d12-renderer through host-owned presentation; the native 2D sprite package proof adds --require-native-2d-sprites so cooked scene sprite texture/material identity, atlas-backed scene sprite planning counters (sprite_batch_plan_atlas_backed_batches, sprite_batch_plan_repeated_atlas_batches, sprite_batch_plan_repeated_atlas_sprites), selected sprite_batch_budget_* world/UI/effects budget profile counters, and HUD submission flow through the host-owned native RHI sprite overlay path with renderer-owned native sprite batch execution counters. The sprite atlas source authoring target records reviewed RGBA8 frame rows plus selected page policy, pivot, and slice-border metadata for plan_sprite_atlas_source_authoring, emits GameEngine.TextureSource.v1 plus GameEngine.SourceAssetRegistry.v1 authoring files, and keeps those source files outside runtimePackageFiles before cooked runtime consumption. The sprite animation package proof adds a first-party cooked sprite_animation payload and --require-sprite-animation so deterministic flipbook ticks through advance_runtime_sprite_flipbook emit sprite_flipbook_frames_sampled and sprite_flipbook_frames_applied counters before sprite frame application emits sprite_animation_frames_sampled and sprite_animation_frames_applied counters. The tilemap runtime UX proof uses first-party GameEngine.Tilemap.v1 metadata and --require-tilemap-runtime-ux so visible tile cells emit tilemap_cells_sampled and tilemap_diagnostics counters without claiming runtime image decoding, production atlas packing, or full tilemap editor UX. The gameplay systems package proof uses --require-gameplay-systems and --require-procedural-generation so 2D PhysicsWorld contacts/triggers, NavigationGrid path/agent movement, AI perception blackboard, behavior authoring validation, quest/dialogue state progression, quest/dialogue save-state validation, runtime inventory item pickup/state validation/recipe completion, reviewed construction placement validation plus runtime-scene placement intent planning, reviewed procedural generation plus procedural placement intent planning, behavior tree counters, and inputContextRebinding counters emit gameplay_systems_* plus input_context_rebinding_ready=1, input_rebinding_profile_overlays_applied=2, captured input_rebinding_action_capture_status/input_rebinding_axis_capture_status, input_rebinding_focus_retained=1, input_rebinding_presentation_rows=2, input_rebinding_glyph_lookup_keys=5, and input_rebinding_diagnostics=0 fields including gameplay_systems_behavior_authoring_diagnostics, gameplay_systems_behavior_authoring_trace_nodes, gameplay_systems_quest_dialogue_transition_rows, gameplay_systems_quest_dialogue_action_ids, gameplay_systems_quest_dialogue_reward_ids, gameplay_systems_quest_dialogue_state_rows, gameplay_systems_inventory_items_transition_rows, gameplay_systems_inventory_items_final_workbench_quantity, gameplay_systems_construction_placement_validation_rows, gameplay_systems_construction_placement_intent_rows, gameplay_systems_construction_placement_intent_accepted_rows, gameplay_systems_construction_placement_intent_occupied_cells, gameplay_systems_procedural_generation_rows, gameplay_systems_procedural_generation_object_rows, gameplay_systems_procedural_generation_encounter_rows, gameplay_systems_procedural_generation_loot_rows, gameplay_systems_procedural_generation_replay_hash, and gameplay_systems_procedural_generation_package_visible_rows. The runtime profile resume package proof uses --require-runtime-profile-resume so plan_runtime_session_profile_resume emits runtime_profile_resume_ready, loaded/defaulted document counters, and save/settings schema version evidence. The runtime menu HUD package proof uses --require-runtime-menu-hud so plan_runtime_menu_hud emits runtime_menu_hud_ready=1, runtime_menu_hud_display_rows=6, runtime_menu_hud_command_rows=2, runtime_menu_hud_dialogue_rows=1, and runtime_menu_hud_input_binding_prompt_rows=1. The audio gameplay mixer package proof uses --require-audio-gameplay-mixer over the cooked jump audio payload so plan_gameplay_audio_mix and AudioMixer emit audio_gameplay_mixer_ready=1, bus/cue/trigger/command, paused/faded bus, looping/spatial command, render, sample-sum, and sample diagnostic counters without native device handles. The audio production proof emits audio_production_* counters with audio_production_status=host_evidence_required, selected package evidence ready, device and HRTF host evidence unavailable, zero codec/background/middleware/HRTF/device-callback/device-IO side effects, two host-gate diagnostics, and a positive replay hash without broad codec support, middleware parity, HRTF execution parity, background streaming execution, native handles, or general production audio quality. The sprite effects particles package proof uses --require-sprite-effects-particles so plan_runtime_sprite_effect_particles emits deterministic sprite_effect_particles_* counters for spawn events, spawned/surviving/render rows, submitted rows, clean diagnostics, and budget diagnostic evidence without damage-number glyphs, GPU particles, scene mutation, renderer/RHI ownership, or native handles. The entity scale/culling package proof uses --require-entity-scale-culling so plan_runtime_entity_scale_culling emits deterministic package-visible entity_scale_culling_* counters for planned rows, visible/culled rows, LOD rows, update buckets, projected draw/update costs, budget-protected rows, clean diagnostics, and budget diagnostic evidence without renderer/RHI mutation. The networking foundation policy proof uses --require-networking-foundation-policy so plan_runtime_network_foundation emits deterministic networking_foundation_* counters for reviewed session rows, transport rows, replication channel rows, replay prerequisite rows, rejected unsafe transport requirements, insecure remote transport diagnostics, and clean reviewed-policy diagnostics without opening sockets, adding transport middleware, performing encryption/authentication handshakes, matchmaking, rollback/prediction, NAT traversal, package mutation, or native handle exposure. The simulation orchestration package proof uses --require-simulation-orchestration so plan_runtime_simulation_orchestration emits deterministic simulation_orchestration_* counters for planned fixed-step rows, ordered command playback rows, budget-limited frames, invalid command diagnostics, consumed/remaining time, and clean reviewed-loop diagnostics without running game rules, pacing renderer frames, implementing rollback/lockstep, mutating packages, or exposing native handles. The gameplay authoring review package proof uses --require-gameplay-authoring-review so review_gameplay_authoring_request emits deterministic gameplay_authoring_review_* counters for accepted feature rows, mutation ledger rows, remediation rows, unsupported capability/recipe/package evidence diagnostics, and unsupported broad-authoring claim diagnostics without applying fixes, mutating cooked packages, executing commands, or claiming autonomous commercial game design. The production authoring workflow proof uses --require-production-authoring-workflows so review_production_authoring_workflow emits deterministic production_authoring_workflow_* counters for six reviewed scene placement, quest/dialogue, item/economy, AI behavior, world region, and validation repair workflow rows, six mutation-ledger rows, and one validation-repair row that targets installed-2d-production-authoring-workflows-smoke while proving shared surface mutation, arbitrary shell, cooked package mutation, native/backend terms, and non-game target paths fail closed without file mutation, package IO, or command execution. The runtime UI workbench proof uses --require-runtime-ui-workbench so plan_runtime_ui_workbench emits runtime_ui_workbench_status=ready, runtime_ui_workbench_ready=1, runtime_ui_workbench_panels=5, runtime_ui_workbench_table_columns=3, runtime_ui_workbench_table_rows=2, runtime_ui_workbench_graph_series=2, runtime_ui_workbench_item_rows=3, runtime_ui_workbench_inventory_rows=1, runtime_ui_workbench_equipment_rows=1, runtime_ui_workbench_shop_rows=1, runtime_ui_workbench_text_inputs=1, runtime_ui_workbench_platform_text_input_requests=1, runtime_ui_workbench_focus_edges=4, runtime_ui_workbench_localization_refs=17, runtime_ui_workbench_localization_identity_ready=1, runtime_ui_workbench_accessibility_refs=11, runtime_ui_workbench_accessibility_identity_ready=1, runtime_ui_workbench_renderer_submission=0, runtime_ui_workbench_text_shaping=0, runtime_ui_workbench_font_rasterization=0, runtime_ui_workbench_ime_sessions=0, runtime_ui_workbench_accessibility_bridge=0, runtime_ui_workbench_image_decoding=0, runtime_ui_workbench_native_platform=0, and runtime_ui_workbench_diagnostics=0 without renderer submission, text shaping, font rasterization, IME session execution, OS accessibility publication, image decoding, native platform work, UI middleware, or native handles. backendReadiness.graphics: NullRenderer fallback plus host-gated D3D12 package window smoke, native 2D sprite package proof with atlas-backed scene sprite plan counters, selected world/UI/effects sprite batch budget profile counters, and renderer-owned native sprite batch execution counters, and Vulkan package window smoke through packaged shader artifacts. backendReadiness.ui: mirakana_ui-headless renderer submission plus Runtime UI Workbench package evidence through --require-runtime-ui-workbench, Runtime UI Production Stack selected package evidence through --require-runtime-ui-production-stack, and cooked UI atlas renderer handoff counters through --require-runtime-ui-renderer-atlas-handoff
